definition: To initiate conversation in a social situation
example: A joke can help break the ice at parties.

word: hit the nail on the head
definition: To describe exactly what is causing a situation
example: You hit the nail on the head with that analysis.

definition: To endure a painful situation with courage
example: I had to bite the bullet and tell the truth.

definition: To work late into the night
example: She burned the midnight oil to finish her thesis.

definition: Feeling ill or unwell
example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.

word: the ball is in your court
definition: It's your turn to make a decision
example: I've made my offer — the ball is in your court.

word: cost an arm and a leg
definition: To be very expensive
example: That designer bag costs an arm and a leg.

definition: Very rarely
example: I only see her once in a blue moon.

definition: To reveal a secret
example: Come on, spill the beans — what happened?

definition: Something that seems bad but turns out to be good
example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.

definition: To avoid talking about the main topic
example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me.

word: let the cat out of the bag
definition: To accidentally reveal a secret
example: He let the cat out of the bag about the surprise party.

definition: In a risky or dangerous situation
example: He's on thin ice after being late three times.

definition: A small part of a much bigger problem
example: These complaints are just the tip of the iceberg.

definition: Making a wrong assumption about something
example: If you think I did it, you're barking up the wrong tree.

definition: Something very easy to do
example: The exam was a piece of cake.

definition: To destroy a relationship or possibility of return
example: Don't burn bridges — you might need that contact later.

definition: To do something in the easiest or cheapest way
example: They cut corners on safety to save money.

definition: To become difficult to control
example: The situation got out of hand quickly.

definition: To make more effort than expected
example: She always goes the extra mile for her clients.
